chief_complaint: A 56-year-old man of South Asian/Pakistani descent with a history of diabetes mellitus and hypertension presented with post-coronavirus disease pneumonia and an anterior abdominal wall lesion.
history: The patient was diagnosed with post-coronavirus disease pneumonia and had an anterior abdominal wall lesion that did not resolve after treatment for cutaneous mucormycosis. He had a history of diabetes mellitus and hypertension, and was administered liposomal amphotericin B intravenously and underwent surgical wound debridement.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the case report.
labs: Histopathology confirmed cutaneous mucormycosis, and polymerase chain reaction testing confirmed cytomegalovirus reactivation.
correct_diagnosis: Coronavirus disease 2019-associated mucormycosis and cytomegalovirus infection
differentials: Coronavirus disease 2019-associated mucormycosis and cytomegalovirus infection
correct_ranking: Coronavirus disease 2019-associated mucormycosis and cytomegalovirus infection
